human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-and-beverage manufacturing specificity, source quality, hold and traceability boundary discipline, supplier escalation posture, commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to food-safety evidence review, traceability conflict analysis, hold and release-boundary handling, supplier-food-safety escalation, and incident packaging inside food-and-beverage manufacturing.
Authority sources cover FDA preventive-controls and traceability regulations, FDA supplier and traceability workflow guidance, Codex hygiene guidance, and GS1 traceability structure.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat incidents, control bypass requests, traceability conflicts, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, recall-authority escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/food-safety-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, procurement, inventory, and logistics ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final release, recall command, supplier substitution authority, and unsupported hold overrides.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated food-and-beverage manufacturing food-safety specialist that supports evidence-backed hold, release-boundary, incident, supplier, and traceability workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
